title: Text Inspection, Search, and Structured Transformation · Operations Case: Text Inspection
summary: Extract a defensible incident summary from mixed logs without silently corrupting delimiters, encodings, ordering, or duplicate policy. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux text inspection increment in a tested text-processing pipeline with fixtures. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Select cat, less, head, tail, wc, file, and od from file size, format, encoding, and inspection goal.
Use command substitution and grouping without losing newlines, status, scope, or observable failure evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Predict Text Inspection
steps.instruction:

content.code:
LC_ALL=C awk -F, 'NR > 1 { print $2, $1 }' data/incidents.csv \
  | sort -k1,1 -k2,2 \
  | uniq -c
